Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Dec-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nicole Webb, Esq., counsel for James Gaither, Reed Gaither, and Stanley Gaither, Co-Trustees (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes the supplemental pleadings filed and that it finds the $325 per hour consultant fee generally acceptable. The Court is concerned that no bond has been filed as of yet for which Ms. Webb offers explanation. The Court remains concerned about the requested $150,000 distribution from the blocked account for "operating" expenses. Ms. Webb reports that this figure is a maximum anticipated amount required for payment of the consultant's fees and for the general administrative efforts to identify worthy charitable recipients of the trust.

After further discussion, the Court will require more specific terms for the distribution of trust funds, either by way of noticed petition or ex parte application.

The Court continues the matter to November 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for the filing of the required bond but otherwise takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

The Court noted concerns regarding the lack of certain specific representations in the petition, particularly with regard to the proposed consultant's purpose, fees, and any associations with the trustees or their families. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #55, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Mills for James Gaither, Reed Gaither and Stanley Gaither (Petitioners).

The Court expressed concern with respect the large amount of fees, amounts paid to Silicon Valley Bank for preparation of taxes and accountings, billings for internal conferences between the Attorney's, the trustee fees, etc. Ms. Mills stated that the 55th accounting petition was outside the parameters than what they usually do for the trust, that there was an ex parte petition that required an appearance, that the funds being in a blocked account causes delay in payment of the taxes, that the tax burden shifted last year and that there was a confluence of events. Ms. Mills also stated that there were two continuances, that there was an issue with two Judges recusing themselves, that Court appearances required internal discussion regarding the Probate Examiner's notes as well as discussion between the Attorney, the trustees and the CPA, and that the trustee fee issue was not typically called into question until last year. In addition, Ms. Mills stated that the trustees are not accountants, that there is $4,500,000.00 in the trust and they use a CPA for tax reporting, that the trustees want to make sure they hire good help and that they have continually followed what the Court has allowed. Furthermore, Ms. Mills stated that they are happy to reduce their fees if it pleases the Court and that because tax preparation is tied in with accountings, there is some efficiency in having Silicon Valley Bank do the accountings. The Fifty Fifth Account and Report, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
